untranslatable_reason: Signal requires real-time earnings announcement data and comparison of actual earnings vs analyst expectations. These are fundamental event-driven data points not available in the allowed indicator set, which contains only price/volume/technical indicators. Earnings surprises require access to earnings calendar data, actual reported earnings, and consensus analyst estimates—none of which are technical indicators.
translation_notes: This is a post-earnings-announcement-drift (PEAD) strategy that fundamentally depends on earnings surprise detection. The allowed indicators are purely technical/price-based and do not include earnings data, earnings surprises, or fundamental event flags. A proper implementation would require: (1) an earnings calendar feed, (2) actual EPS data, (3) consensus analyst estimates, and (4) surprise magnitude calculation. The placeholder conditions above are non-functional and demonstrate why untranslatable=true is appropriate.
